The Senate reconciliation bill, known as the One Big Beautiful Bill Act (OBBBA), was passed by the House and extends many provisions of the 2017 Tax Cuts and Jobs Act (TCJA) using a 'current policy' baseline. This bill was signed into law by President Trump on July 4, 2025. The bill prevents tax increases scheduled for 2025 by making individual tax cuts permanent.

The One Big Beautiful Bill Act (OBBBA) signed into law by President Trump on July 4, 2025, modifies the Inflation Reduction Act (IRA) to make domestic content requirements mandatory for obtaining the IRA's Production Tax Credits (45Y). The OBBBA harmonizes domestic content requirements under Section 48E with those under Section 45Y for projects beginning construction on or after June 16, 2025. This change effectively makes meeting domestic content rules a condition for receiving the 45Y Production Tax Credit.

The One Big Beautiful Bill Act, signed by President Trump on July 4, 2025, amends several Inflation Reduction Act (IRA) tax credits, including the Investment Tax Credits (ITC) under section 48E. The Act specifies domestic content percentages for the clean electricity investment credit, making these requirements mandatory for obtaining the ITC. For example, it sets a domestic content percentage of 45% for projects starting construction after June 16, 2025, and before January 1, 2026, and 50% for projects starting construction in 2026.

The House of Representatives passed a reconciliation bill (H.R. 1) on May 22, 2025, which amends several Inflation Reduction Act (IRA) tax credits, including the Advanced Manufacturing Production Credit (Section 45X). The bill introduces new Foreign Entity of Concern (FEOC) restrictions that prohibit credits for taxpayers identified as specified foreign entities or foreign-influenced entities and disallow credits for projects receiving material assistance from prohibited foreign entities after December 31, 2025. This effectively requires the use of domestic materials or subcomponents for the Advanced Manufacturing Production Tax Credits (45X) starting in 2025.
